message:
fix(channels): handle poisoned mutexes gracefully to prevent double-panics during unwinding

In both broadcast and watch channels, mutex lock calls used `.expect()`
which would trigger a second panic when a mutex was already poisoned
during stack unwinding (e.g., when a prior panic left the lock poisoned).
This change replaces `.expect()` with `let Ok(...) = ... else { return }`
so that drop paths and waker-notification paths fail open instead of
cascading panics.

Broadcast channel (broadcast.rs):
- Recv::clear_waiter_registration now returns early on poisoned mutex
  instead of panicking, preventing double-panic when dropping a pending
  Recv future after the channel's inner mutex was poisoned
- Added test: recv_drop_on_poisoned_mutex_does_not_panic — intentionally
  poisons the mutex then drops a pending recv future to verify no panic

Watch channel (watch.rs):
- WatchInner::wake_all_waiters now returns early on poisoned waiters
  mutex, allowing send() and send_modify() to complete the value update
  even when waker notification cannot proceed
- Added test: send_and_modify_on_poisoned_waiters_mutex_do_not_panic —
  poisons the waiters mutex then verifies send() and send_modify() both
  succeed without panicking and correctly update the stored value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
